parameters.name: group
parameters.label: Group
parameters.description: If there are input groups, specifying a group name in this field will cause this to act only upon the group specified. Accepts patterns, as described in .

parameters.name: method
parameters.label: Method
parameters.description: Select the method of projection for the Ray .

parameters.name: dotrans
parameters.label: Transform Points
parameters.description: If selected, it will transform the input points as defined below. Leave this off when only interested in updating the source point attributes.

parameters.name: lookfar
parameters.label: Intersect Farthest Surface
parameters.description: If selected, this option allows the user to choose between intersecting with the closest intersecting object or the furthest. See example, below.

parameters.name: normal
parameters.label: Point Intersection Normal
parameters.description: If selected, updates each point in the source geometry with the normal at the collision surface it intersects with. If the point doesn't intersect at the collision surface, a normal of (0,0,0) is used

parameters.name: bounces
parameters.label: Bounces
parameters.description: The number of times to bounce the ray off of the collision surface before creating the output position. For example, if bounces is set to 1, then the point will be projected onto surface at the first

parameters.name: bouncegeo
parameters.label: Save Bounce Geometry
parameters.description: When enabled, the projected geometry will be saved each time the projected ray bounces off a surface resulting in multiple copies of the input geometry. See the second Ray snippet for an example.

parameters.name: putdist
parameters.label: Point Intersection Distance
parameters.description: If selected, updates each point intersected with the distance to the collision surface. If the point doesn't intersect at the collision surface a distance of 0 is used. This value is placed in the poi

parameters.name: scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description: A value of zero will leave the input point unaffected. A value of one will land the point on the intersection surface. Negative values and values > 1 are also valid.

parameters.name: lift
parameters.label: Lift
parameters.description: This value further offsets the surface input by offsetting it in the direction of its normal.

parameters.name: sample
parameters.label: Sample
parameters.description: This value determines the number of rays sent per point. If greater than one, the remaining rays are perturbed randomly, and averaged.

parameters.name: jitter
parameters.label: Jitter Scale
parameters.description: Controls the perturbation of the extra sample rays.

parameters.name: seed
parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description: Allows a different random sequence at higher sampling rates.

parameters.name: newgrp
parameters.label: Create Point Group
parameters.description: If selected, it will create a point group containing all the points which were intersected successfully.

parameters.name: hitgrp
parameters.label: Ray Hit Group
parameters.description: Specifies the name of the above point group.
